content: Chronic replacement: hydrocortisone 15-25mg/day in 2-3 divided doses (mimics diurnal rhythm; higher morning dose); alternatives prednisone 5mg or dexamethasone 0.5mg daily. Primary AI requires mineralocorticoid: fludrocortisone 0.05-0.2mg daily, titrated to renin, blood pressure, electrolytes. Stress dosing essential: minor illness/injury - double/triple oral dose; moderate stress (fever >38°C, dental procedures) - hydrocortisone 50mg IV then 25mg q6-8h; major stress (surgery, trauma, sepsis) - hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus then 50mg q6h or 200mg/day continuous infusion. Adrenal crisis management: immediate hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus, then 50mg q6h; aggressive IV fluids (normal saline with dextrose if hypoglycemic); treat precipitating cause; transition to oral maintenance over 2-3 days. Patient education critical: medical alert bracelet, emergency injection kit, never stop abruptly, sick-day rules.
